body:
### Why this matters

Azure Database for PostgreSQL currently lists PostgreSQL 11, 12, and 13 as Extended Support versions. Extended-support engines are older major versions and should be upgraded before they become harder or more expensive to operate.

### What this control checks

This control finds PostgreSQL Flexible Servers whose engine version starts with `11`, `12`, or `13`. The recommended target is PostgreSQL 14 or newer, with PostgreSQL 16, 17, or 18 preferred when compatible.

### Operational note

PostgreSQL major upgrades require testing extensions, query plans, connection poolers, logical replication, backups, and application compatibility before production rollout.
name: Ensure Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Servers are not on extended-support versions
description: Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Servers running PostgreSQL 11, 12, or 13 extended-support versions
timedControlSummary: Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Servers on PostgreSQL 11, 12, or 13 should be upgraded from extended-support versions.
timedControlDisruptionRisk: Leaving PostgreSQL on extended-support versions increases security, compatibility, and future upgrade risk.
remediationDescription:
## Azure remediation

> **Service-wide PostgreSQL upgrade program (recommended):** upgrade all PostgreSQL Flexible Servers on extended-support versions by application group. This gives teams one tested plan for extensions, parameter changes, read replicas, connection pooling, and application compatibility.

### Azure Portal

1. Open **Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible servers**.
2. Select the affected server and review backups, maintenance window, extensions, replicas, and application compatibility.
3. Use the **Major version upgrade** feature to upgrade to a supported version such as PostgreSQL 14 or newer.
4. Validate extensions, query plans, connection pools, and application migrations.

### Azure CLI

Inspect the current version:

```bash
az postgres flexible-server show \
  --resource-group <resource-group> \
  --name <server-name> \
  --query '{name:name,version:version,state:state}'
```

Start a major version upgrade after testing:

```bash
az postgres flexible-server upgrade \
  --resource-group <resource-group> \
  --name <server-name> \
  --version <target-major-version>
```

### References

- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/postgresql/configure-maintain/concepts-supported-versions

timedControlRules.summary: Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Servers on PostgreSQL 11, 12, or 13 should be upgraded from extended-support versions.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: Leaving PostgreSQL on extended-support versions increases security, compatibility, and future upgrade risk.
timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Upgrade to PostgreSQL 14 or newer, preferably PostgreSQL 16, 17, or 18 after compatibility testing.
